title: CHALLENGES OF LOW SENSITIVITY OF THE DAS-ELISA METHOD BASED ON POLYCLONAL ANTIBODIES IN THE DIAGNOSTICS OF THE PATHOGENS CAUSING BLACK LEG AND SOFT ROT OF POTATOES
author: Maria Yerokhova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Bacteria from genera Pectobacterium and Dickeya were obtained the status of Recommended Regulated Non-Quarantine Pests (RRNQPs) on seed potatoes in EPPO in 2018. Currently, the isolation of bacteria on semi-selective media and then the identification by the different molecular methods (modifications of PCR, etc) are applied to diagnose the pathogens (Pectobacterium and Dickeya), causing blackleg and soft rot of potatoes in the most of countries. According to scientific publications, immunological methods (DAS-ELISA with polyclonal antibodies) are universally applied in 1980-1990s. Nowadays, DAS-ELISA to diagnose the causative agents of blackleg of potatoes is applied less due to its low sensivity and specifity in the most of countries, but in Russia, only this method is practically being used in last 10 years. To improve the detection of pathogens, causing blackleg and soft rot of potatoes, by immunological methods, TAS-ELISA or Enrichment DAS-ELISA are worldwide applied.
steps_raw: Bacteria from genera Pectobacterium and Dickeya were obtained the status of Recommended Regulated Non-Quarantine Pests (RRNQPs) on seed potatoes in EPPO in 2018. Currently, the isolation of bacteria on semi-selective media and then the identification by the different molecular methods (modifications of PCR, etc) are applied to diagnose the pathogens (Pectobacterium and Dickeya), causing blackleg and soft rot of potatoes in the most of countries. According to scientific publications, immunological methods (DAS-ELISA with polyclonal antibodies) are universally applied in 1980-1990s. Nowadays, DAS-ELISA to diagnose the causative agents of blackleg of potatoes is applied less due to its low sensivity and specifity in the most of countries, but in Russia, only this method is practically being used in last 10 years. To improve the detection of pathogens, causing blackleg and soft rot of potatoes, by immunological methods, TAS-ELISA or Enrichment DAS-ELISA are worldwide applied.
